title: Position Reconciliation on Restart
summary: Defines how Decifer reconciles its in-memory position state with IBKR on bot startup or after a crash-restart, preventing duplicate orders or missed exits.
source_of_truth: IBKR portfolio (ib.portfolio(account)) — always wins over in-memory active_trades dict.
triggers: bot.py startup — reconcile_with_ibkr() called once before first scan cycle
After successful reconnect — reconcile_with_ibkr() called in _reconnect_worker() on_success path
reconciliation_steps: 2. Build composite keys: symbol for stocks; symbol_right_strike_expiry for options
3. For each IBKR position NOT in active_trades: validate price (3-way: IBKR + yfinance + TradingView), then add to active_trades
4. For each active_trades entry NOT in IBKR: remove from active_trades (assumed manually closed or liquidated during downtime)
5. Log all additions and removals at WARNING level

trade_history_reconciliation.strategy: Group IBKR execution fills by (symbol, orderId); consolidate partial fills using weighted-average price
trade_history_reconciliation.deduplication: Check exec_id first; then fuzzy match (symbol, side, qty, fill_price, fill_time within 1 min); skip if already logged

thread_safety: _trades_lock (RLock) guards all reads and writes to active_trades. Network calls (ib.portfolio, price validation) happen outside the lock to prevent blocking.
options_handling: IBKR reports option averageCost per-contract (×100 multiplier). Reconciliation converts to per-share. marketPrice is already per-share. Composite key includes right (C/P), strike, and expiry to prevent symbol collisions.
known_gaps: If bot crashes mid-fill of a partial order, the fill may be double-counted. Dedup logic mitigates but does not eliminate this for options with complex fill sequences.
Reconciliation runs at startup and after reconnect — not continuously. A position opened between reconnect and next reconciliation could be missed until the next startup.
3-way price validation can fail if all three providers are down simultaneously — in that case, the position is added with a 0 price and flagged for manual review.
